behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires proving an externally visible side effect (the rejected row is actually present in the dead-letter queue with correct row metadata), which triggers the System round-trip rule. Unit tests of validator logic cannot prove queue write/read correctness across boundaries. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because the behavior text contains no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against a real test S3 object containing rows with missing required fields, then read back from the real DLQ endpoint/topic and assert each rejected item includes the original CSV row number and payload.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is pure transformation logic (input date string -> canonical output) and does not require real infrastructure or model behavior. Higher levels add little new reality signal for this specific behavior. Rejected Integration/System because behavior text is scoped to conversion correctness, not module handoff or external side effects.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for the date conversion function using representative ISO 8601, US, and European date inputs (including edge cases) and asserting exact canonical output.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This is a runtime/resource behavior (timeout + memory envelope) that depends on real process/OS characteristics and full pipeline execution. In-process unit/integration fakes can miss allocator, buffering, and I/O effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML decision chain is involved in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline end-to-end with a generated 500k-row CSV in a production-like runtime configuration, measure wall-clock completion vs configured timeout, and monitor peak RSS/heap to verify no OOM and acceptable memory headroom.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly depends on an external error-path contract from S3 (503 transient failure) and correct handling at that boundary, which is a System-triad trigger. Mocks can hide real SDK/HTTP retryability nuances and backoff timing behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real or controlled HTTP/S3-compatible endpoint that emits transient 503 responses, run a batch and assert retry attempts occur with increasing backoff intervals and that processing eventually continues without whole-batch failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving durable external side effects across process restart and DB round-trip observation (no duplicate persisted rows), which cannot be validated by unit-only checks. Restart behavior plus real PostgreSQL state consistency pushes this to System. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because the behavior text has no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against real PostgreSQL, force a mid-batch restart/crash, resume processing, then query the target table to verify exactly-once/idempotent outcomes (no duplicate primary/business keys among already committed rows).
